text: Research whether PLTR has had a genuinely material new government or enterprise contract catalyst in the last 30 days. Use public-source tools, cite URLs, separate Evidence -> Belief update -> Conclusion, and do not recommend or execute a trade. Keep it concise but source-backed.

# PLTR Contract Catalysts — Last 30 Days (2026-04-04 → 2026-05-04)

## Evidence

**1. USDA $300M Blanket Purchase Agreement — Apr 22, 2026 (clearest, most-cited catalyst)**
- Joint USDA/Palantir press release: $300M BPA supporting the National Farm Security Action Plan (NFSAP), built on Palantir's existing "Landmark" platform; backs "One Farmer, One File" and $11B Farmer Bridge Assistance Program rollout. [Businesswire release](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260422128086/en/USDA-and-Palantir-Launch-Partnership-to-Deliver-Faster-Modernized-Support-for-Farmers)
- Independent coverage: [Reuters via Yahoo UK](https://uk.finance.yahoo.com/news/usda-palantir-sign-300-million-135846683.html), [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/22/palantir-inks-300-million-deal-with-usda-to-safeguard-food-supply.html), [GovConWire](https://www.govconwire.com/articles/usda-palantir-bpa-national-farm-security), [The Register](https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/23/palantir_wins_us_department_of_agriculture_contract/) (includes USDA CIO Sam Berry and CDAO Christopher Alvares on record; USDA sole-source justification cited vs. Databricks/Snowflake/IBM/SAS/Salesforce/Alteryx).
- Counterparty: USDA (FPAC mission area). Value: $300M ceiling, BPA structure (task-order draw-down, not guaranteed revenue). Builds on prior work — expansion, not net-new agency.

**2. Cleveland-Cliffs — 3-year enterprise AI deal — Apr 28, 2026**
- [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-28/steelmaker-cliffs-taps-palantir-technologies-for-ai-overhaul): CLF signed a 3-year agreement to deploy Palantir across production planning, order entry, day-to-day operations. CEO Lourenco Goncalves on record post-pilot. Corroborated by [TMC Insight](https://insight.tmcnet.com/insight/cleveland-cliffs-secures-three-year-deal-with-palantir-to-integrate-ai-across-steelmaking-operations-moizghla).
- Counterparty: CLF (NYSE-listed enterprise). Dollar value: **not disclosed** — material risk flag for sizing.

**3. Golden Dome software consortium inclusion — Apr 22, 2026**
- [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-22/spacex-tapped-for-industry-group-developing-golden-dome-software): Palantir named alongside SpaceX, Anduril, Aalyria and others in the group building the Golden Dome C2/software layer. This is participation in the software consortium, **not** a discrete award amount for PLTR. Note: the separate $3.2B Space-Based Interceptor OTAs announced Apr 24–25 went to 12 firms — **Palantir is not on that SBI list** ([DefenseScoop](https://defensescoop.com/2026/04/24/golden-dome-space-based-interceptor-missile-defense-contractors/), [Fortune/Bloomberg](https://fortune.com/2026/04/25/spacex-anduril-lockheed-raytheon-northrop-golden-dome-interceptor-contracts/)).

**4. Prior items referenced but dated outside the 30-day window**
- $385.4M VA contract and $10B Army Enterprise Agreement (consolidation of 75 contracts) are mentioned in April 2026 coverage as context but were awarded earlier. Not fresh catalysts.
- Maven Smart System ceiling raise to ~$1.3B (May 2025) — older.

## Belief update

The branch-law escalation bar for PLTR (per `branch_moqsxtsl` — "cited government or enterprise contract catalyst, renewal, or procurement framework") is now **met** on a sourced-evidence basis, which it was not in the prior heartbeat:

- USDA $300M BPA: named counterparty, named dollar ceiling, named procurement vehicle (BPA), multiple independent sources, direct on-the-record agency officials, official press release on Businesswire. This is a textbook "cited government procurement framework."
- CLF deal: named counterparty, multi-year term, Bloomberg-sourced — but **no disclosed dollar value**, so it's materially softer evidence for sizing a thesis.
- Golden Dome software consortium: qualitative inclusion, no discrete contract value attributed to PLTR in the last 30 days.

Relative to the prior debate (where Bull punted at 0.18–0.22 confidence with no cited counterparty), evidence quality has clearly improved. However:
- A BPA is a **ceiling**, not a booking. The $300M should not be treated as recognized revenue; task orders against it will determine actual flow-through.
- The USDA relationship is an **expansion** of an existing Landmark engagement, not a new logo, which partially tempers the incremental-growth narrative.
- No dollar figure disclosed for Cliffs limits how much enterprise-diversification credit can be taken.
- Timing is close to PLTR's upcoming earnings; headline already in the tape, so any "catalyst" framing has to separate announcement-beta from earnings-beta.

## Conclusion

Yes — within the last 30 days PLTR has had **at least one genuinely material, well-sourced government contract catalyst** (USDA $300M BPA, Apr 22, 2026) and **one enterprise contract** (Cleveland-Cliffs 3-year AI deal, Apr 28, 2026) with undisclosed value. These are publicly cited from the counterparty, corroborated by independent outlets, and satisfy the named-counterparty / named-vehicle source-quality test that was missing in the earlier heartbeat. Golden Dome software-consortium inclusion adds qualitative tailwind but no fresh dollar figure for PLTR specifically.

Per instructions, no trade is recommended or executed. Key residual uncertainties to watch: (a) task-order draw-down rate against the USDA BPA ceiling, (b) CLF deal contract value (likely disclosed on PLTR's next earnings call or CLF's), and (c) whether any of this is already priced in heading into PLTR's next earnings report.
reasoning:
I need to search for Palantir contract catalysts over the past month, looking at announcements and developments between early April and early May 2026. Now I'm verifying the $300M USDA BPA and need to cross-check the VA $385.4M contract details, look into the Golden Dome reference, and scan for any recent enterprise commercial agreements from the last month. I'm pulling together the key events from the past month that drove this movement. There's the USDA's $300 million contract award announced on April 22nd across major financial outlets, and then Cleveland-Cliffs announced a significant three-year AI partnership deal on April 28th.

I'm also seeing Palantir's involvement in the Pentagon's Golden Dome software initiative from that same April 22nd Bloomberg report, plus references to a $10 billion Army Enterprise Agreement mentioned in an April 27th article, though that consolidation likely happened earlier. The VA contract is in the mix too, though the timing on that one is less clear. These are the concrete, well-sourced catalysts that satisfy the requirement for documented government or enterprise contract announcements.

toolCalls.summary: Loaded 0 Supermemory snippets. Loaded 2 informative branch profiles.

Kairos notification.trading_message.threshold_notify: branch=branch_moqsxtsl: run=3667f3cb-e96c-458c-9e63-4fcc3694ff39: Manual PLTR contract-catalyst debate with a thin, source-less heartbeat package. Bull's strongest point was merely procedural — it twice punted at very low confidence (0.18, 0.22), unable to name a contract, counterparty, value, or source. Bear's strongest point (0.85) was that the escalation directly violates the branch law's explicit source-quality requirement: no cited government or enterprise contract, renewal, or procurement framework was provided, and PLTR routinely generates low-signal contract chatter that gets overhyped. No tool searches were executed to surface a catalyst. Net read: the law's escalation bar is unmet, the package is thin and effectively uncontested on the material question, and no trade intent is warranted. Portfolio holds no PLTR position, so sell is not applicable; buying without a sourced catalyst would contradict the law.

input.query: Palantir PLTR new government contract award announcement

results.title: Palantir inks $300 million deal with USDA to safeguard food supply
results.author: Samantha Subin,Seema Mody
highlights:
Palantir inks $300 million deal with USDA to safeguard food supply
[...]
- Palantir announced a $300 million deal with the USDA, which will use the company’s software in farmland management.
- Farmers face rising supply costs spurred by the war in Iran and an ongoing trade war with key partners, including China.
- Palantir is diversifying beyond its well-known defense contracts but has also faced backlash for working with both ICE and DHS.
[...]
Palantir announced a $300 million deal with the U.S. Department of Agriculture, which will use the software company’s technology to manage farmland as geopolitical risks threaten global supply chains.
[...]
China, a
[...]
Palantir was founded in 2003 to scale U.S. defense capabilities in
[...]
wake of 9/11, and CEO Alex Karp has long touted the company’s commitment to supporting U.S. warfighters. The company has recently gained recognition for its AI-powered Maven Smart System platform, which was used by the U.S. military in Iran.
results.summary: Palantir won a $300 million USDA contract to help manage farmland with its software, signaling broader government adoption beyond defense and a potential boost to Palantir’s growth amid ongoing geopolitical and supply-chain pressures facing farmers. The deal comes as farmers contend with rising costs from the Iran war, trade tensions with China, and fertilizer/shipping disruptions, highlighting Palantir’s expanding role in critical infrastructure and data-driven agriculture.

results.title: USDA, Palantir Sign $300M BPA to Support Farm Security
results.author: Miles Jamison
highlights:
The U.S. Department of Agriculture has partnered with Palantir Technologies under a $300 million blanket purchase agreement, or BPA, to support the National Farm Security Action Plan and enhance service delivery to U.S. farmers.
[...]
providing operational software to
[...]
Built on the Palantir-powered Landmark platform, USDA previously supported the launch of the Farmer Bridge Assistance Program, which saw strong early online enrollment and delivered billions in payments within days. The platform integrates legacy systems into a secure, unified foundation, lowering maintenance needs, strengthening data security and allowing faster response to evolving priorities.
[...]
The USDA deal adds to Palantir’s growing federal work, including its role in developing software for the $185 billion Golden Dome missile defense system and a $385.4 million contract awarded by the Department of Veterans Affairs.
results.summary: The market-relevant headline is: "USDA, Palantir Sign $300M BPA to Support Farm Security." Catalyst: Palantir provides operational software under a $300 million BPA to modernize USDA farm services, enhance security, and fight fraud, positioning Palantir as a key vendor in the USDA’s One Farmer, One File modernization effort and broader national-security-linked farm protection initiatives.

results.summary: Palantir won a $300 million contract with the USDA to modernize digital services for farm programs, aiming to speed payments, improve fraud detection, and enable data sharing across systems as part of the “One Farmer, One File” initiative. This government deal reinforces Palantir’s growth in the public sector and could drive near-term revenue and investor optimism ahead of the next earnings release.

results.title: USDA and Palantir sign $300 million software purchase agreement
results.author: Reuters    Updated  Wed 22 April 2026 at 4:24 pm CEST   1 min read
highlights:
April 22 (Reuters) - The U.S. Department of Agriculture and software company ‌Palantir Technologies (PLTR) on Wednesday announced ‌the signing of a $300 million Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA).
[...]
The ​agreement is aimed at supporting the National Farm Security Action Plan (NFSAP), the release said, adding that it will ‌also modernize ⁠how USDA delivers services to America's farmers.
[...]
The ​BPA will build ‌on Palantir's existing work with USDA's "Landmark" platform, and will support the "One Farmer, One File" initiative. The statement noted that Landmark is enabling USDA to ‌transform how farmers ​report acreage through self-service ​digital tools.
[...]
"Protecting ​America's farmland is protecting ‌America itself, and this work ​gives ​USDA the visibility and speed needed to safeguard our food supply," ​said USDA ‌Chief Information Officer Sam Berry.
results.summary: USDA and Palantir signed a $300 million Blanket Purchase Agreement to modernize farm services and support the National Farm Security Action Plan, building on Palantir’s Landmark platform to improve farmers’ self-service reporting and delivery of USDA services. This collaboration could act as a market-positive catalyst for Palantir as it expands its government deployments and operational software footprint.

results.title: Palantir makes $300 deal with USDA to boost farm security with AI
highlights:
The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) signed a $300 million blanket purchase agreement with AI company Palantir Technologies to update the government agency’s digital infrastructure and address the goals of the National Farm Security Action Plan.
[...]
Previously, the USDA worked with Palantir on its Landmark platform, which was used to send one-time bridge payments to farmers, sending $4.4 billion in five days, Palantir shared.
[...]
“Protecting America’s farmland is protecting America itself, and this work gives USDA the visibility and speed needed to safeguard our food supply. Our farmers sustain this nation, and modern tools help us support them with greater precision. I look forward to working with Palantir as we continue serving the American farming community, which serves all of us every single day,” said Sam Berry, USDA chief information officer, in a press release.
[...]
Ali Monfre, federal
[...]
lead at Palantir, added,
[...]
, and we are honored to partner in
results.summary:
Summary:
Palantir and USDA ink a $300 million blanket purchase agreement to modernize the agency’s digital infrastructure, aiming to secure farmland, boost supply-chain resilience, and reduce fraud via AI, building on prior Palantir-enabled programs like Landmark. The deal supports USDA’s One Farmer, One File initiative to streamline farmer admin tasks and faster payments.

This contract acts as a market-moving catalyst for Palantir’s government software footprint and near-term revenue potential from USDA programs.

results.title: USDA and Palantir sign $300 million software purchase agreement - The Economic Times

The US Department of Agriculture and software company Palantir Technologies on Wednesday announced the signing of a $300 million Blanket Purchase Agreement(BPA).The agreement is ‌aimed at ⁠supporting ⁠the National Farm Security Action Plan (NFSAP), the release said, adding that it will also modernize how USDA delivers services to America's farmers.Palantir said it will provide ⁠operational software ‌to enable USDA to improve service delivery for farmers⁠and government field staff.

results.summary: The US Department of Agriculture and Palantir have signed a $300 million Blanket Purchase Agreement to modernize USDA service delivery to farmers, building on the Landmark platform to advance the “One Farmer, One File” initiative and support the National Farm Security Action Plan. This deal could accelerate digital modernization in agricultural services and enhance data-driven decision-making for farmers and USDA staff.

results.title: USDA and Palantir Launch Partnership to Deliver Faster, Modernized Support for Farmers
highlights:
The US Department of Agriculture (USDA) and Palantir Technologies Inc. have signed a $300 million Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA) to support the National Farm Security Action Plan (NFSAP) and modernize how USDA delivers services to farmers. The partnership aims to strengthen farm security as a national security imperative and accelerate producer-first program delivery across USDA’s Farm Production and Conservation (FPAC) mission areas. Palantar will provide operational software to enable USDA to improve service delivery for American farmers and government field staff. This partnership builds on existing work with USDA‘Landmark platform powered by PalantIR, which backed the rollout of the $11 billion Farmer Bridge Assistance Program in February.
[...]
--- Release Versions --- ### Contacts
[...]
, media@palantir.
results.summary: USDA and Palantir have signed a $300 million Blanket Purchase Agreement to modernize farm services, strengthen farm security as a national priority, and accelerate producer-first program delivery across FPAC. The partnership builds on the Landmark platform and the One Farmer, One File initiative to cut red tape, speed payments, and improve visibility and fraud protection in agricultural programs.

results.title: Palantir Just Landed Its Next $300 Million Deal | The Motley Fool
results.author: Danny Vena, CPA
highlights:
Palantir struck an important agreement with the U.S. Department of Agriculture.
[...]
The $300 million software deal expands the existing relationship between the two.
[...]
In a press release that dropped on Wednesday morning, Palantir announced a $300 million purchase agreement with the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA).
[...]
The deal builds on the existing partnership between the USDA and Palantir, which produced the USDA's Landmark software platform, backed by Palantir, and the rollout of the $11 billion Farmer Bridge Assistance Program in February.
[...]
Palantir had a breakout year in 2025. Revenue of $4.5 billion grew 56%, accelerating throughout the year. U.S. commercial revenue -- fueled by adoption of Palantir's AIP -- grew 109%, while U.S. government revenue climbed 55%. Earnings per share (EPS) of $0.63 surged 163%, and the company's remaining performance obligation -- contractually obligated revenue that hasn't yet been recognized -- soared 143% to $4.2 billion.
results.summary: Palantir landed a $300 million government deal with the USDA to modernize its operations and support the One Farmer, One File initiative, expanding a growing government-focused revenue stream and highlighting Palantir’s enterprise and AI platforms alongside its public-sector strength. This deal acts as a meaningful catalyst, reinforcing Palantir’s diversified revenue beyond its commercial segments.

results.title: USDA partners with Palantir to modernized support for farmers - Blue Book
results.author: Greg Johnson
highlights:
April 22, 2026 MIAMI–(BUSINESS WIRE)–The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) and Palantir Technologies Inc. announced the signing of a $300 million Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA) to support the National Farm Security Action Plan (NFSAP) and modernize how USDA delivers services to America’s farmers.
[...]
“Protecting America’s farmland is protecting America itself, and this work gives USDA the visibility and speed needed to safeguard our food supply,” said USDA Chief Information Officer Sam Berry. “Our farmers sustain this nation, and modern tools help us support them with greater precision. I look forward to working with Palantir as we continue serving the American farming community, which serves all of us every single day.”
results.summary: USDA and Palantir signed a $300 million Blanket Purchase Agreement to modernize farm support and bolster national farm security under the National Farm Security Action Plan, integrating Palantir’s Landmark platform to accelerate farmer-first services and reduce program red tape. This signals a significant public-sector tech modernization catalyst with potential implications for agricultural subsidies, disaster aid delivery, and tighter protection against fraud and supply-chain risks.

The deal comes as farmers face higher costs from global tensions and trade pressures, underscoring Palantir’s growing market-relevant footprint in agriculture and national security-adjacent sectors.

results.title: Palantir signs $300 million USDA software deal
results.author: Cris Tolomia      Wed, April 22, 2026 at 9:05 AM MDT   2 min read
highlights:
The U.S. Department of Agriculture and Palantir Technologies signed a $300 million Blanket Purchase Agreement aimed at modernizing how the agency delivers services to American farmers and addressing farmland security concerns.
[...]
Under the terms of the deal, Palantir will deploy operational software designed to streamline how field staff and farmers receive support from the agency, with the partnership tied to the broader National Farm Security Action Plan. Palantir has previously worked with the USDA on a platform called "Landmark," through which the agency has shifted acreage reporting to a digital, self-service model for farmers. It will also support the agency's "One Farmer, One File" initiative.

Palantir was founded in 2003 and has built much of its business on U.S. government contracts, including defense work. The USDA agreement extends the company's footprint beyond military applications into agricultural policy and food supply management.
results.summary: Palantir won a $300 million USDA Blanket Purchase Agreement to modernize and streamline federal farmer services under the National Farm Security Action Plan, extending its government footprint into agricultural policy and food supply management. This deal could act as a near-term growth catalyst by expanding Palantir’s government software deployments and reinforcing data-driven farm support and security initiatives.

results.title: VA seeks single contractor to modernize sprawling supply chain
highlights: The Veterans Affairs Department (VA) is seeking a single-award contract to modernize and integrate its sprawling supply chain management systems. The department is seeking to replace 63 legacy systems that touch 174 sites across the country. The contract will likely be a hybrid fixed-price award with optional tasks. The VA wants a single contractor to support the department's Healthcare Environment and Logistics Management product line, which has 34 products currently in operation. The technical demands include continuous integration and support for the DevSecOps software practice. The new supply chain system will need to integrate with other enterprise-wide VA programs.
results.summary: The market-relevant headline: The VA is pursuing a single-award contract to modernize and integrate 63 legacy supply-chain systems across 174 sites, targeting a unified, enterprise-wide system with DevSecOps, cloud hosting (Platform One/OpenShift), and AI/ML capabilities. Catalyst: VA’s plan to consolidate fragmented operations into an integrated system via a five-year contract, emphasizing modernization, standardization, and enterprise-wide interoperability with other VA programs.

The deal aligns with the USDA’s National Farm Security Action Plan and the “One Farmer, One File” initiative, signaling potential upside for Palantir as the agency digitizes farmer support and land data.

agency’s analysts.

results.title: Palantir secures USDA contract to consolidate farmer data, modernize programs | General | farmweeknow.com
results.author: TODD NEELEY, DTN ENVIRONMENTAL EDITOR
highlights:
USDA signed a $300 million deal with Palantir Technologies Inc. this week to help the agency implement its "One Farmer, One File" initiative announced in February to help farmers reduce paperwork.
[...]
The deal announced with Palantir is expected to help modernize how the agency delivers services to farmers. The agreement is designed to strengthen farm security to accelerate the delivery of farmer programs in USDA's farm production and conservation areas, according to a news release from Palantir.
[...]
Palantir said the latest agreement "builds on existing work" with USDA's Landmark platform powered by Palantir. That platform backed the rollout of the $11 billion Farmer Bridge Assistance (FBA) program in February.
[...]
U.S. Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins touted the initiative at Commodity Classic in February, when the agency launched FBA enrollment during which over 35,000 signups were tallied in a few days.
results.summary: Palantir has secured a $300 million USDA contract to consolidate farmer data and modernize service delivery under the “One Farmer, One File” initiative, leveraging the Landmark platform to streamline programs, reduce paperwork, and bolster farm security. This deal signals faster, more unified access to USDA programs for farmers and could impact government tech modernization and agricultural supply-chain resilience.

results.title: How Palantir merged 75 contracts into a $10B Pentagon lock - Pravda NATO
highlights:
In 2018, the US Army Research Lab signed a modest R&D contract with Palantir on artificial intelligence. Eight years later, 75 contracts have been merged into a single $10 billion deal, and the company's war platform has become an official Pentagon program — now the standard for the entire US war machine.
[...]
The Army awarded Palantir a $250 million firm, fixed-price contract for AI and machine learning research and development, valid through September 2026. The contract continued work stemming from the previous year's agreement with the Army Research Lab.
[...]
Seventy-five active contracts were consolidated into a single ten-year Enterprise Agreement with a $10 billion ceiling — the largest deal in Palantir's history. The consolidation placed Palantir alongside Boeing, Lockheed Martin, and Raytheon in the upper tier of defense contractors.
results.summary: Summary: The article traces Palantir’s progression from a CIA-backed startup to securing a $10B, 10-year Enterprise Agreement consolidating 75 defense contracts, making its war platform a core Pentagon program and expanding its global footprint with NATO and UK adoption—raising competition and procurement concerns hierover.

results.title: Cleveland-Cliffs Secures Three-Year Deal With Palantir to Integrate AI Across Steelmaking Operations | TMC Insight
highlights: Lourenco Goncalves, Chairman, President, and CEO of Cleveland-Cliffs, stated that after completing initial pilot work, the impact was a gamechanger and that Palantir's technology allows the company to solve problems in ways humans cannot. His comments also touched on cultural and policy alignment between the two organizations, which is a subtle but important factor. AI implementations tend to fail not because of technology, but because of workflow disruption, training gaps, or mismatched expectations around data governance. The fact that leadership emphasized alignment suggests the two sides have already invested time in harmonizing their approaches.
results.summary: Cleveland-Cliffs inks a three-year Palantir deal to embed enterprise AI across its steelmaking operations, from production planning to real-time site coordination, signaling a major modernization push in heavy industry. The move positions Cliffs to better integrate data, anticipate constraints, and optimize multi-site throughput as AI adoption accelerates in manufacturing.

highlights:

The partnership aims to strengthen farm security as a national security imperative and accelerate producer-first program delivery across USDA’s Farm Production and Conservation (FPAC) mission areas. Palantar will provide operational software to enable USDA to improve service delivery for American farmers and government field staff. This partnership builds on existing work with USDA‘Landmark platform powered by PalantIR, which backed the rollout of the $11 billion Farmer Bridge Assistance Program in February.

, media@palantir.

The catalyst is the expanded partnership and the rapid, digital-first rollout across FPAC programs, aiming to improve service delivery, cut time-to-payment, and enhance resilience of the food supply chain.

results.title: Space Force names 12 companies to develop Golden Dome’s space-based interceptors | DefenseScoop
results.author: measley
highlights:
The Space Force’s acquisition arm announced on Friday the 12 companies who have received contracts to develop space-based interceptor (SBI) prototypes for President Donald Trump’s Golden Dome.
[...]
The service awarded other transaction authority (OTA) agreements — worth up to a combined $3.2 billion — to the vendors in late 2025 and early 2026, according to a Space Systems Command press release. Under the contracts, the companies will develop prototypes of a space-based architecture that can shoot down enemy missiles after they’re launched.
[...]
Prototypes developed under the SBI program are slated for demonstration and integration into the Golden Dome architecture by 2028, Col. Bryon McClain, program executive officer for space combat power, said in a statement.
[...]
“Adversary capabilities are advancing rapidly, and our acquisition strategies must move even faster to counter
results.summary:
Summary:
The Space Force awarded OTAs totaling up to $3.2 billion to 12 companies (including Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, SpaceX, Raytheon, Anduril, and Booz Allen Hamilton) to develop prototypes for space-based interceptors as part of the Golden Dome missile-defense architecture, with initial demonstrations targeted by 2028 and full deployment aimed in the mid-2030s.

results.title: Space Force tasks a dozen companies for Golden Dome space-based interceptors - Breaking Defense
results.author: Michael Marrow
highlights:
In a press release today, the Space Force revealed that 20 contracts — collectively totaling a potential $3.2 billion — have been awarded to the 12 firms over the last several months under a dedicated SBI program. According to the Space Force, the companies will help construct a proliferated low earth orbit “constellation of interceptors capable of boost, midcourse and glide phase engagements.” Essentially, those systems would be capable of knocking down missiles, including hypersonic glide vehicles capable of maneuvering, throughout earlier phases of flight.
[...]
The announcement comes just one day after the Golden Dome’s czar, Space Force Gen. Michael Guetlein, emphasized that the Pentagon needs to show progress to the American public for the project estimated to cost $185 billion. Guetlein also recently testified before lawmakers that the SBI concept may not make it into the final Golden Dome architecture if it is shown to be prohibitively costly.
results.summary:
Summary:
Space Force has awarded 20 contracts worth up to $3.2 billion to 12 companies to develop space-based interceptors (SBIs) for the Golden Dome missile defense shield, aiming for an initial capability demonstration in 2028 amid concerns about cost and affordability.

results.title: SpaceX, Anduril among companies to win Golden Dome contracts | Fortune
results.author: Bloomberg
highlights:
SpaceX, Anduril among companies to win Golden Dome contracts | Fortune
[...]
# SpaceX, Anduril among companies to win Golden Dome contracts
[...]
April 25, 2026, 5:44 PM ET
[...]
The US Space Force awarded 12 companies, including Lockheed Martin Corp. and SpaceX, contracts worth up to $3.2 billion to develop prototypes for space-based interceptors under President Donald Trump’s Golden Dome plan.
results.summary: The Space Force awarded up to $3.2 billion in contracts to 12 companies, including Lockheed Martin and SpaceX, to develop prototypes for space-based interceptors under President Trump’s Golden Dome plan. This signals a push toward rapid defense capabilities and potential future procurement in space defense.

results.title: Pentagon Taps 12 Companies for Golden Dome SBI Tech
results.author: Jacqueline Feldscher
highlights:
The Pentagon announced on Friday that it had tapped a dozen companies to build the space-based interceptor (SBI) component of the Golden Dome missile-defense system.
[...]
Though the awards were announced last week, SSC shared that the other transition authority (OTA) agreements were actually awarded late last year and early this year. The 20 awards to 12 companies are worth up to $3.2B combined.
[...]
“Utilizing Other Transaction Authority agreements, we attracted both traditional and non-traditional vendors, while harnessing American innovation, and ensuring continuous competition,” Col. Bryon McClain, the Space Force’s program executive officer for space combat power, said in a statement. “With the commitment and collaboration of these industry partners, the Space Force will demonstrate an initial capability in 2028.”
results.summary: The Pentagon awarded 12 companies, including SpaceX, Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, and Anduril, up to $3.2B to develop the space-based interceptor component of the Golden Dome missile-defense system, with initial capability targeted for 2028. This marks a broad blend of traditional and non-traditional vendors under OTA agreements to advance a commercially-driven, space-based defense architecture.

results.title: DoD outlines progress on Golden Dome missile defense architecture | Military Aerospace
results.author: Jamie Whitney
highlights:
VIRGINIA BEACH, Va. - Senior leaders from the U.S. Department of Defense (DoD), military services, and industry gathered at Joint Expeditionary Base Little Creek-Fort Story in Virginia on 23 April to provide an update on Golden Dome for America (GDA), a developing architecture for homeland missile defense.
[...]
Program officials cited several recent milestones, including completion of an initial architecture blueprint, establishment of a Command-and-Control Consortium, and the award of contracts for key system components. The consortium is expected to support a distributed C2 environment involving multiple vendors, consistent with broader DoD efforts toward modular, interoperable battle management systems.
results.summary:
Summary:
The DoD’s Golden Dome outlines a layered, automated missile-defense architecture that integrates space-based sensing, interceptors, and C2 to counter ballistic, hypersonic, and cruise missiles, with milestones including a completed architecture blueprint, a Command-and-Control Consortium, and key component contracts leveraging AI, space assets, and open architecture. The initiative signals a market driver toward interoperable, distributed defense systems and next-generation interceptors.

results.title: SpaceX Tapped for Industry Group Developing Golden Dome ...
results.author: Sana Pashankar
highlights:
SpaceX Joins Group Building Pentagon's Golden Dome Defense Software - Bloomberg
[...]
April 22, 2026 at 6:20 PM UTC
[...]
SpaceX is part of a group of companies developing the operating system underpinning President Donald Trump’s Golden Dome initiative, according to people familiar with the matter, underscoring the deep ties between Elon Musk’s rocket-and-satellite maker and the US government.
[...]
The company is focused on integrating satellite communications for military operations, said the people, who asked not to be identified because they weren’t authorized to speak publicly. SpaceX is working alongside eight other defense and artificial intelligence companies, including Anduril Industries Inc., Palantir Technologies Inc. and Aalyria Technologies Inc., to build software connecting missile defense capabilities, according to one of the people.
results.summary: SpaceX is joining a group of defense and AI companies to develop the Pentagon’s Golden Dome software, focusing on integrating satellite communications for military operations and connecting missile-defense capabilities. This collaboration highlights SpaceX’s deep ties with the U.S. government and positions the company as a key supplier in a high-sophistication defense platform.
